sourceMovie.title: Frequency
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Frequency works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Source Code
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Frequency clicked for you, Source Code (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: A Quiet Place Part II
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Frequency clicked for you, A Quiet Place Part II (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Frequency?
faq.answer: Try Source Code, A Quiet Place Part II, Déjà Vu—each shares DNA with Frequency while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Frequency?
faq.answer: Frequency (2000) is best described as Science Fiction, Thriller, Family, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Frequency worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
